mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows month-over-month growth below 0.2%, the zero-lower-bound regime with subdued price pressures sustains equity valuations through low discount rates and reduced real rate uncertainty, driving positive 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-2008 QE regime with near-zero rates and muted inflation
regime_hint: zero-lower-bound monetary accommodation with subdued inflation
failure_condition: Trigger condition met in >= 15 instances over 3-year window but positive 20-day returns occur in < 55% of triggered periods, or Rank IC < 0.05 in triggered regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.2 percentage points signals accelerating labor market tightening that raises corporate wage pressures and reduces profit margins, producing negative 20-day forward returns as equity risk premiums widen.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines > 0.2pp month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Labor market tightening typically precedes wage acceleration and margin compression
failure_condition: Trigger condition met in >= 10 instances but negative 20-day returns occur in < 50% of triggered periods, or correlation between UNRATE momentum and 20-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 500k annualized units, signaling weakening consumer and residential demand that portends lower corporate earnings growth and negative equity returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0.5% AND HOUST < 500k units annualized
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales and housing are leading demand indicators for corporate earnings
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown with synchronized retail and residential weakness
failure_condition: Trigger condition met in >= 8 instances over 3-year window but positive 60-day returns occur in > 45% of triggered periods, or Rank IC < 0.04 in triggered regime.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) exhibits a 3-month rolling decline of more than 1.5% while remaining above the prior-year level, indicating supply-side weakness without demand collapse that compresses profit margins and generates negative 5-day forward returns as investors reprrice earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month rolling change < -1.5% AND INDPRO year-over-year change > 0%
evidence_refs: fact:Supply-side weakness with positive year-over-year growth signals margin compression rather than demand destruction
failure_condition: Trigger condition met in >= 12 instances but negative 5-day returns occur in < 52% of triggered periods, or mean 5-day return in triggered periods is not significantly different from baseline (t-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth accelerates above 200k month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) remains stable or rises, indicating a shift toward lower-quality job creation that fails to support consumer confidence and equity valuations, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 200k AND UNRATE month-over-month change >= 0pp
evidence_refs: fact:Divergence between payroll growth and unemployment improvement suggests labor market quality deterioration
failure_condition: Trigger condition met in >= 10 instances but positive 20-day returns occur in > 50% of triggered periods, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.20).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) remain above 550k annualized units while real GDP growth (GDPC1) is positive and accelerating quarter-over-quarter, indicating sustained demand-side momentum that supports corporate earnings growth and equity valuations, producing positive 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing and GDP growth are coincident indicators of demand-side strength
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 60-day returns occur in < 55% of periods when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above 0.5% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 0.3% month-over-month, signaling the onset of monetary tightening in an inflationary environment that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth > 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:Monetary tightening combined with inflation acceleration historically precedes equity drawdowns
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising inflation
failure_condition: Trigger condition met in >= 8 instances but negative 20-day returns occur in < 55% of triggered periods, or Rank IC < 0.05 in triggered regime.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) exhibits positive year-over-year growth above 2% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows month-over-month by more than 150k, indicating robust supply-side capacity expansion paired with strong labor demand that supports sustained corporate earnings and positive 5-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Supply-side strength with robust employment growth supports earnings expansion
regime_hint: expansion with synchronized supply and labor strength
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 5-day returns occur in < 53% of periods when both conditions are met.
